source.section: 2.12 - AGM-84H SLAM-ER Cruise Missile > 2.12.3 - Target Designation > 2.12.3.1 - TOO / Waypoint

steps.action: Press OSB next to MODE to toggle between TOO (Target-of-Opportunity) and PP (Pre-Planned) target designation modes. In this case, we will choose TOO.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to MODE to select TOO target designation mode.

steps.action: Press OSB next to MSN to enter the Mission Parameters page.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to MSN to enter the Mission Parameters page.

steps.action: We can define up to two Targets of Opportunity per missile station: TOO1 and TOO2. We will designate the target on TOO1. Press OSB next to TOO1 to select it.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to TOO1 to select the first target of opportunity.

steps.action: The ORP (Offset Release Point) is the designated target point (A/G TGT). For now, the field is empty since no target is defined yet.
steps.voiced: Confirm ORP field is empty — no target is designated yet.

steps.action: (Optional) If you want to set TERM (Terminal Attack) parameters such as heading (HDG), attack angle (ANG) and velocity (VEL), press OSB next to TOO UFC. You will be able to enter them via the UFC. However, since we will control the missile remotely during the terminal attack stage, these parameters are not required and we will leave them blank,
steps.voiced: Optionally set TERM attack parameters via TOO UFC — leave blank for remote terminal control.

steps.action: (Optional) We do not need O/S (Offset) parameters to the ORP since we will control the missile remotely during the terminal attack stage. Therefore, we will not need to use O/S UFC function. Parameters for Station 8
steps.voiced: Confirm O/S offset parameters are not required — leave blank for remote terminal control.

steps.action: In this example, Waypoint 4 is set directly on our target. It is good practice to cross-check the waypoint elevation with the F10 map.
steps.voiced: Confirm target waypoint coordinates and elevation against the F10 map.

steps.action: On the HSI (Horizontal Situation Indicator), press OSB to select WYPT (boxed when selected). After, select Waypoint 4 using the OSBs next to the arrows.
steps.voiced: On the HSI, press OSB to select WYPT, then use the arrows to select the target waypoint.

steps.action: Once the Waypoint 4 is selected, press the OSB next to WPSDG (Waypoint Designate) to designate Waypoint 4 as your TGT1 (Target Point 1). On the HSI, « WYPT » will switch to « TGT ». Minimum and Maximum Launch Range circles will appear on the HSI.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to WPSDG to designate the waypoint as TGT1 — confirm WYPT switches to TGT.

steps.action: The coordinates and elevation of the designated waypoint (TGT1) will then directly transfer to the ORP (Offset Release Point) in the MSN page.
steps.voiced: Confirm waypoint coordinates transfer to the ORP in the MSN page.

steps.action: The target designation diamond, steering cues, range to target, SLAM-ER indication, TMR indication (time required to be within range of a valid SLAM-ER launch) and TOO indication will appear on the HUD.
steps.voiced: Confirm designation diamond, steering cues, and SLAM-ER/TMR indications appear on the HUD.

steps.action: Press the OSB next to RETURN to return back to the SMS (Stores) page. We are now almost ready to begin our strike and launch the missile.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to RETURN to go back to the SMS page.

steps.action: We can select up to five intermediate steerpoints for the missile to fly through. In our case, we will select Waypoint 1, Waypoint 2 and Waypoint 3. Waypoint 4 is located on the target. For simplicity, all waypoint coordinates are already stored in our database.
steps.voiced: Confirm steerpoint waypoints are loaded — up to five intermediate steerpoints can be programmed.

steps.action: Press OSB next to STP (Steerpoint). Once STP is boxed, the DIST parameter (distance from target when seeker head goes active) can be modified from the UFC panel.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to STP to enable steerpoints — DIST becomes editable on the UFC.

steps.action: Once STP option is selected (boxed), the UFC (Up-Front Control) will display STP1 (Intermediate Steerpoint #1). Press OSB next to STP1.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to STP1 on the UFC.

steps.action: Press OSB next to WYPT to enter the Waypoint Menu, then enter the Waypoint Number you want to use as a reference for the first Steerpoint on the UFC keypad (“1”), then press “ENT”.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to WYPT, enter the waypoint number for STP1 on the UFC keypad, then press ENT.

steps.action: STP2 (Intermediate Steerpoint #2) field will appear once STP1 is set. Repeat the two previous steps to associate Waypoint #2 with STP2 and Waypoint #3 with STP3. When STP2 and STP3 are set, STP4 field will become visible but with no associated waypoint, which is fine.
steps.voiced: Repeat for STP2 and STP3 — associate each with the next sequential waypoint.
